MARCInd1Label: Type of personal name entry element
Justification for Mapping:
If there are only name part subfields and subfield $0/$1 we can just add statements with the external IRI as subject, the RDA aap element, and the name part as a string; there is no point in using the nomen approach because its purpose is to identify the scheme and this is already identified by the IRI. Example:
<work> has subject person https://id.worldcat.org/fast/01583751 . // ind1=1 and only name part subfields $a and $d are recorded
https://id.worldcat.org/fast/01583751 has authorized access point for person "Bickford, Sarah Gammon, 1855-1931" . // only name part subfields $a and $d are recorded--Gordon
If we have no qualifier subfields, then AP/AAP is relevant, and it is useful to add the RDA AAP statement to the subfield $1 IRI as subject, with the value as a string object, no nomen, because we already 'know' the source from the IRI.
Transformation Notes:
<work> rdawo:P10261 <$1> . // has subject person
<$1> nomen property <nomenIRI>
<nomenIRI> rdan:P80068 stripEndStop(concatenateOA($a, $b, $c, $d, $g, $j, $q $u))” . // has nomen string
<nomenIRI> rdan:P80069 <scheme> .// has scheme of nomen

If $2 is approved source, use rdaao:P50411 .// has authorized access point for person. If unapproved use rdaao:P50377 .// has access point for person
- CP 2024-12-02
